skill.name: meta-abstract-screener
skill.description: Screens research papers based on title/abstract and inclusion criteria, providing a structured Yes/No/Maybe decision. Use when you need to filter literature for meta-analysis or systematic reviews.
skill.summary: Automates title and abstract screening against inclusion criteria for systematic reviews and meta-analyses.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 54 static analysis findings are false positives. The weak cryptographic algorithm findings correspond to lines with no cryptographic code (comments, license metadata, audit data). The shell backtick execution findings are markdown code fence formatting, not shell injection. System reconnaissance findings are standard Python import sys and print statements. The hardcoded URL is a legitimate source repository citation. The skill is a legitimate academic screening tool with no malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Confused as Shell Backtick Execution
medium_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 33 instances of backtick characters as Ruby/shell command execution. All instances are standard markdown code fence or inline code formatting. No actual command substitution or shell injection exists in these files. This is a pattern-based false positive from the static analyzer.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detection - False Positive
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 19 high-severity findings for weak cryptographic algorithms. None of the referenced lines contain any cryptographic code. Affected lines include license metadata (SKILL.md:3), skill description text (SKILL.md:11), comments (scripts/screen_paper.py:38), and audit metadata (meta-abstract-screener_audit_result_v2.json:72). All are false positives from the automated scanner.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Detection - False Positive
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 8 instances of system reconnaissance. These are standard Python import sys statements and print() calls used for error handling in the JSON validation script. The script only processes command-line arguments and validates JSON structure. No system enumeration or data collection occurs.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Detection - False Positive
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged a URL in SKILL.md:7 as a hardcoded network address. This is a legitimate source repository citation pointing to the skill's GitHub repository (github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills). It is a documentation reference, not a malicious network call.

content.user_title: Screen Research Papers for Systematic Reviews
content.value_statement: Researchers spend hours manually filtering irrelevant papers during literature reviews. This skill automates title and abstract screening against custom inclusion criteria to produce structured Yes/No/Maybe decisions with detailed reasoning.
actual_capabilities: Analyze paper titles and abstracts against custom inclusion and exclusion criteria
Produce structured Yes/No/Maybe screening decisions with detailed reasoning
Validate screening output format against a JSON schema using a Python validation script
Apply strict rules to exclude systematic reviews and meta-analyses from datasets
Generate reproducible screening results in JSON format for further processing
limitations: Cannot access paid journal paywalls or full-text articles for screening
Requires clearly defined inclusion and exclusion criteria to function correctly
Does not replace human judgment for borderline or complex screening cases
Screening accuracy depends on the quality of provided title and abstract text

use_cases.title: Systematic Literature Review Screening
use_cases.description: A graduate student screens 200 papers from a PubMed search against their PICO inclusion criteria for a thesis.

use_cases.title: Meta-Analysis Study Selection
use_cases.description: A research team filters search results to exclude systematic reviews and meta-analyses before statistical analysis.

use_cases.title: Grant Proposal Literature Check
use_cases.description: A postdoctoral fellow quickly evaluates relevant literature to identify research gaps for a funding application.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Single Paper Screening
prompt_templates.prompt:
Screen this paper for inclusion in a systematic review.

Title: [Paper Title]
Abstract: [Paper Abstract]

Inclusion Criteria: [List criteria]
Exclusion Criteria: [List criteria]

Provide a Yes/No/Maybe decision with detailed reasoning.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user screening a single paper against criteria

prompt_templates.title: Batch Literature Screening
prompt_templates.prompt:
Screen the following papers against these criteria:

Inclusion Criteria: [Criteria]
Exclusion Criteria: [Criteria]

Papers:
1. Title: [Title 1]
   Abstract: [Abstract 1]
2. Title: [Title 2]
   Abstract: [Abstract 2]

For each paper provide Result and Reason in JSON format.
prompt_templates.scenario: User screening multiple papers in one session

prompt_templates.title: Strict Exclusion of Reviews
prompt_templates.prompt:
Screen this paper for a meta-analysis. Strictly exclude any systematic reviews, meta-analyses, or narrative reviews unless otherwise specified.

Title: [Title]
Abstract: [Abstract]

Criteria: [Inclusion and Exclusion criteria]

Output as JSON with Result and Reason fields.
prompt_templates.scenario: Meta-analysis requiring exclusion of other review articles

prompt_templates.title: Validation with Python Script
prompt_templates.prompt:
Screen the paper and then validate the output format.

Title: [Title]
Abstract: [Abstract]

First produce the screening result as a JSON object with Result and Reason fields.
Then run: python scripts/screen_paper.py '<json_output>' to validate the output schema.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user wanting programmatic validation of results

output_examples.input:
Paper Title: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy for Insomnia in Older Adults
Abstract: This randomized controlled trial examined CBT-I efficacy in 120 adults aged 65+ with chronic insomnia.
Inclusion Criteria: RCT, adults 65+, insomnia diagnosis
Exclusion Criteria: Systematic reviews, non-English
output_examples.output:
Result: Yes
Reason: This is an RCT (not a review) studying CBT-I in adults aged 65+ with insomnia, matching all inclusion criteria.

output_examples.input:
Paper Title: Insomnia Treatments: A Systematic Review of Recent Literature
Abstract: We reviewed 45 studies examining pharmacological and non-pharmacological interventions for insomnia.
Inclusion Criteria: Primary research, adults, insomnia treatment
Exclusion Criteria: Systematic reviews or meta-analyses
output_examples.output:
Result: No
Reason: This paper is a systematic review and must be excluded per the meta-analysis screening rules.

output_examples.input:
Paper Title: Sleep Quality and Cognitive Function in Aging Populations
Abstract: This cross-sectional study examined relationships between sleep quality and cognitive test scores in 300 community-dwelling older adults.
Inclusion Criteria: Primary research, adults 60+, examines sleep
Exclusion Criteria: None specified
output_examples.output:
Result: Maybe
Reason: The paper involves sleep and older adults but does not specify an insomnia intervention. More detail is needed to confirm relevance.

best_practices: Define inclusion and exclusion criteria clearly before screening to ensure consistent results across all papers.
Review the reference prompts in references/screening_prompts.md for guidance on handling borderline cases.
Validate JSON output format using the included Python validation script for programmatic consistency.
anti_patterns: Including systematic reviews or meta-analyses in a dataset intended to exclude them, which skews results.
Accepting screening decisions without reviewing the reasoning for Maybe or borderline results.
Using the skill without providing clearly defined inclusion and exclusion criteria, leading to inconsistent output.

faq.question: What types of research papers can this skill screen?
faq.answer: This skill screens any paper with a title and abstract. It works best for systematic reviews and meta-analyses in any research domain.

faq.question: Does this skill access full-text articles?
faq.answer: No. The skill only analyzes titles and abstracts provided by the user. It cannot access paywalls or full-text content.

faq.question: What do Yes, No, and Maybe decisions mean?
faq.answer: Yes means the paper meets all inclusion criteria. No means it clearly does not qualify. Maybe means more information is needed for a final decision.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for non-medical literature reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. The screening logic works for any research domain that uses inclusion and exclusion criteria to filter papers.

faq.question: How does the validation script work?
faq.answer: The Python script screen_paper.py validates the screening output JSON to enforce the correct Result and Reason schema.

faq.question: Why does the skill exclude systematic reviews by default?
faq.answer: Systematic reviews are excluded by default because including them in a new meta-analysis would double-count existing synthesized results.
